front: Short Story: narrator dreams he is in a movie theater watching a film of his parents' courtship
Short Story: wakes on his twenty-first birthday
Short Story: describes ocean waves "somersaulting and arching their backs so beautifully, showing white veins in the green and black"
Short Story: parents visit Coney Island boardwalk
Short Story: title taken from an epigraph to a W.B. Yeats poetry book
Short Story: inspired by the broken marriage of the author's parents
Poem: describes an "inescapable animal" that "moves where I move, distorting my gesture" — the "hungry beating brutish one / In love with candy, anger, and sleep"
back: The Heavy Bear Who Goes With Me (Delmore Schwartz)
work: The Heavy Bear Who Goes With Me

front: Poem: epigraph "the withness of the body" from the author's teacher Alfred North Whitehead

work: The Heavy Bear Who Goes With Me

front: Author: inspiration for Von Humboldt Fleisher in Saul Bellow's Humboldt's Gift
Author: died of alcoholism in 1966 at the Chelsea Hotel
Author: dedicatee of twelve elegies in John Berryman's His Toy, His Dream, His Rest
Author: Robert Lowell also wrote a poem remembering his stuffed duck and kitten named Oranges
Author: youngest winner of the Bollingen Prize for collection Summer Knowledge
Author: also wrote the drama Shenandoah; unsuccessful epic Genesis was an early example of confessional poetry (term coined by M.L. Rosenthal)
Author: taught Lou Reed, who dedicated the song 'European Song' on The Velvet Underground and Nico to him
